5.notes: JAX_LOG_COMPILES emits event counts, not timings; per-stage wall-clock timing would require harness-splitting which CONTEXT explicitly rejects.  See per_call_wall_clock for full-pipeline wall-clock times.

20.notes: JAX_LOG_COMPILES emits event counts, not timings; per-stage wall-clock timing would require harness-splitting which CONTEXT explicitly rejects.  See per_call_wall_clock for full-pipeline wall-clock times.

bottleneck_verdict.evidence: The single dominant bottleneck is cache_key — specifically a cache scope vs SLURM scheduler mismatch. The persistent-compilation cache directory is keyed per-node via JAX_COMPILATION_CACHE_DIR=.../.jax_cache_gpu/${SLURMD_NODENAME} (cluster/14_benchmark_gpu.slurm:99), but SLURM does not guarantee node affinity across afterok-chained jobs. The user-pasted cluster listing shows 13 distinct compute-node subdirectories (m3g107/m3g109/m3g111/m3g113/m3g114/m3g115/m3g118/m3g119/m3a118/m3n101/m3n103/m3n109/m3n112) totalling 1.8G; Phase 14 job 54899271 routed its cache into m3a118 per bench14_54899271.out:38, and any subsequent benchmark landed on a fresh node with empty cache and re-compiled from scratch. Quantitative evidence: at P=50, cold_jit_s = 229s and next_proc_warm_s = 229s (ratio 1.00x; jit_scaling_sweep.csv rows 4 and 7); at P=5, cold 55.5s / next_proc_warm 55.5s (1.00x); at P=20, cold 245.7s / next_proc_warm 245.7s (1.00x). The cross-process speedup is 0% at every tested P. Closure-over-data (RESEARCH Risk #5) is FALSIFIED by 21-06 HLO byte-identity: nuts_P5 == pscan_P5_cold == pscan_P5_warm produce identical total_signatures (52ce790fb6831595) and scan_body hashes (2f25aed94588c656), and XLA-emitted cache keys jit_scan-{03f89614, 1ec79288, 6b3ba0a8, 7d77cd68, 9c682c4c, be5a09c2, c450ea14} are bit-identical across cold and warm SLURM jobs. The graph size is also not pathological: PAT-RL 3-level scan body is ~175 inlined ops/trial (21-01), in-range with NumPyro 100-state HMM ~200 ops/step. The in-process tracing cache DOES work (2.0x speedup at P=5, 2.5x at P=50). Candidate 7 (warmup_params duplication) is DISSOLVED by 21-02 Patch C (fit_batch_hierarchical accepts warmup_params, hierarchical.py:2012). The fix surface is cluster/14_benchmark_gpu.slurm (add --nodelist=<node> SBATCH pin + delete N=2 warm-up Phase-1), NOT production src/ code. Follow-up plan: 14.1-07 (node-pin + verify); success criterion next_proc_warm_s/cold_jit_s >= 3.0x at P=50 (currently 1.00x).

hlo_op_counts.notes: Derived from .planning/phases/21-benchmark-bottleneck-diagnosis/benchmark-scope-memo.md §c comparison table. ~2x tolerance on FLOPs extrapolation (1 HLO op ~= 10-100 FLOPs at P=50 depending on tensor shape; CONTEXT-locked tolerance). NUTS-step-count assumption: 32 leapfrog steps/draw midpoint (upper bound 2^max_tree_depth = 1024 at max_tree_depth=10; empirical hierarchical-model range 16-64). Scan-body direct ops are literal StableHLO op count in the jit_scan_body callee; inlined counts include all nested @jit sub-computation depths (continuous_node_posterior_update, posterior_update_precision_continuous_node, posterior_update_mean_continuous_node, precision_update, predict_precision, binary_state_node_prediction, etc.). pick_best_cue scan body is ~18x larger than PAT-RL per trial because pick_best_cue has 3 parallel binary branches sharing one volatility parent (3x the posterior updates per trial). State-space baselines (NumPyro HMM, LGSSM) sourced from RESEARCH.md §Structural comparison table and §BlackJAX Analogous Cases — not locally measured, cited per CONTEXT §Comparison baselines state-space-only scope. P=50 extrapolated total HLO op count range is 2.5-3.5k (scan body shape-invariant × 192 trials + outer graph ~400-800).
